articles.headline: Gulzar Houz Fire Tragedy: Calls for Enhanced Urban Safety Norms and Disaster Preparedness in Hyderabad
articles.explainer:
• A devastating fire at Gulzar Houz in Hyderabad claimed at least 17 lives, primarily impacting commercial establishments and residential areas.
• The incident has sparked a critical debate on urban planning, fire safety regulations, and the enforcement mechanisms in densely populated historic areas.
• Experts highlight the need for modernized infrastructure, strict adherence to building codes, and effective disaster response protocols.
• The tragedy underscores vulnerabilities in old city areas, prompting calls for comprehensive safety audits and public awareness campaigns.
• Government authorities have initiated an inquiry into the cause of the fire and are reviewing existing safety guidelines.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Urbanization and its problems
Infrastructure development
prelimsPoints: Gulzar Houz is a historic fountain in Hyderabad's Old City.
Disaster Management Act, 2005 provides for effective management of disasters.
National Building Code of India (NBC) sets guidelines for building construction and safety.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the challenges in ensuring urban safety and disaster preparedness in India's rapidly growing cities, with reference to recent incidents.
Discuss the role of local bodies, state governments, and central agencies in implementing and enforcing fire safety norms.
Suggest measures to upgrade infrastructure and improve emergency response systems in congested urban areas.
articles.imageDescription: Smoke billowing from the Gulzar Houz market area in Hyderabad following the fire.
articles.trivia: Gulzar Houz, meaning 'fountain of roses', was built in the 17th century by the Qutb Shahi dynasty to provide water to the Charminar area.

articles.headline: M. R. Srinivasan, Architect of India's Nuclear Power Program, Passes Away at 95
articles.explainer:
• Dr. M. R. Srinivasan, a towering figure in India's nuclear energy landscape and former Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission, passed away at the age of 95.
• He played a pivotal role in the design, construction, and operation of India's indigenous nuclear power plants, including the Madras Atomic Power Station (MAPS).
• Srinivasan was instrumental in advancing India's three-stage nuclear power program, emphasizing self-reliance in nuclear technology.
• His leadership was crucial during the challenging phase of international sanctions following India's nuclear tests.
• He received the Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honor, for his contributions to science and engineering.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Energy
Nuclear technology
prelimsPoints: M. R. Srinivasan was Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission (AEC) from 1987 to 1990.
He contributed to the construction of India's first pressurized heavy water reactors (PHWRs).
India's three-stage nuclear power program aims to utilize thorium reserves.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the significance of India's indigenous nuclear power program in achieving energy security and technological self-reliance, with reference to contributions from pioneers like M.R. Srinivasan.
Discuss the current status and future prospects of India's nuclear energy sector, highlighting challenges and opportunities.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Dr. M. R. Srinivasan, a distinguished nuclear scientist.
articles.trivia: Dr. Srinivasan was also a member of the Planning Commission of India.

articles.headline: Renowned Astrophysicist Jayant Narlikar Passes Away, Leaves Legacy in Cosmology Research
articles.explainer:
• Padma Vibhushan recipient and eminent astrophysicist, Professor Jayant V. Narlikar, passed away at the age of 86.
• Known for his pioneering work in cosmology, Narlikar was a strong proponent of the quasi-steady state theory of the universe, developed with Fred Hoyle.
• He founded the Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA) in Pune, fostering astronomical research in India.
• Narlikar was also a prolific science popularizer and writer, making complex scientific concepts accessible to the public through his books and articles.
• His contributions significantly shaped India's presence in the global astrophysics community.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Space
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Jayant Narlikar was associated with the steady-state theory of the universe.
He founded the Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA) in Pune.
The Big Bang theory is the most widely accepted cosmological model.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of cosmological theories, highlighting the contributions of Indian scientists like Jayant Narlikar.
Examine the role of institutions like IUCAA in promoting scientific research and education in India.
articles.imageDescription: Professor Jayant Narlikar, a distinguished astrophysicist.
articles.trivia: Jayant Narlikar studied at the University of Cambridge and was a student of Fred Hoyle.

articles.headline: Former Chief Justice of Karnataka High Court, S. R. Nayak, Dies at 81
articles.explainer:
• Justice S. R. Nayak, a distinguished jurist and former Chief Justice of the Karnataka High Court, passed away at the age of 81.
• Known for his integrity and commitment to justice, he had a long and impactful career in the Indian judiciary.
• Post-retirement, he also served as the Chairman of the Karnataka State Human Rights Commission, advocating for human rights protection.
• His judgments often reflected a deep understanding of constitutional law and social justice principles.
• Justice Nayak's demise marks the loss of a significant voice in India's legal fraternity.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Appointments to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Human Rights
prelimsPoints: High Court judges are appointed by the President of India.
State Human Rights Commissions are statutory bodies.
The Chief Justice of a High Court is appointed under Article 217 of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of former judges in upholding constitutional values and promoting human rights post-retirement.
Examine the functions and challenges faced by State Human Rights Commissions in India.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Justice S. R. Nayak.
articles.trivia: Justice Nayak was also known for his advocacy for legal aid to the underprivileged.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves 'PM-GatiShakti 2.0' for Accelerated Infrastructure Development
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has given its nod to 'PM-GatiShakti 2.0', a comprehensive master plan aimed at accelerating multi-modal connectivity and infrastructure development across India.
• This enhanced version focuses on leveraging advanced technology, particularly AI and geospatial mapping, for integrated planning and project implementation.
• PM-GatiShakti 2.0 seeks to break silos between different ministries and departments, ensuring synchronized project execution and reducing logistical costs.
• The initiative aims to boost economic growth, create employment opportunities, and enhance India's global competitiveness by improving logistical efficiency.
• Special emphasis will be placed on last-mile connectivity and the development of critical infrastructure in underserved regions.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Growth and Development
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: PM-GatiShakti aims for integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure projects.
It uses a GIS-based platform for visualization and planning.
The initiative covers 16 ministries including Railways, Roads, Petroleum & Gas, Power, Telecom, Shipping, Aviation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of integrated infrastructure planning initiatives like PM-GatiShakti in achieving India's economic development goals.
Discuss the challenges in implementing large-scale infrastructure projects in India and how PM-GatiShakti 2.0 aims to address them.
articles.imageDescription: Infographic illustrating the multi-modal connectivity vision of PM-GatiShakti.
articles.trivia: The PM-GatiShakti National Master Plan was launched in October 2021.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Approach Amidst Global Economic Volatility
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI)'s Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• This decision reflects a cautious stance, balancing concerns over persistent inflation with the need to support economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest that the RBI is closely monitoring global commodity prices, geopolitical tensions, and domestic demand conditions.
• The central bank emphasized the resilience of the Indian economy but flagged potential risks from external headwinds.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.
The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting framework in India aims to keep inflation at 4% with a +/- 2% band.
mainsPoints: Examine the role of the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee in managing inflation and fostering economic growth in India.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in conducting monetary policy amidst global economic volatility and domestic supply-side pressures.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) headquarters in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The MPC consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India Pushes for Global South Representation at G20 Finance Ministers' Meeting in Brazil
articles.explainer:
• India's Finance Minister participated in the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting hosted by Brazil, advocating for stronger representation of the Global South.
• The discussions focused on global economic stability, sustainable development financing, and multilateral debt restructuring for developing nations.
• India emphasized the need for inclusive economic policies and equitable access to resources and technology for emerging economies.
• The meeting also addressed challenges posed by climate change financing and the reform of international financial institutions.
• India's stance aligns with its ongoing efforts to amplify the voices and concerns of developing countries on global platforms.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Brazil holds the G20 presidency in 2024.
The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union.
India hosted the G20 Summit in 2023.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role as a voice for the Global South in multilateral forums like the G20.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for developing countries in shaping global economic governance.
Examine the importance of debt restructuring and climate finance for sustainable development in the Global South.
articles.imageDescription: Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors attending the G20 meeting in Brazil.
articles.trivia: The G20 represents about two-thirds of the world’s population, 85% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-08'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its latest Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08) aboard the PSLV-C58 rocket from Sriharikota.
• EOS-08 is equipped with advanced optical and synthetic aperture radar payloads, designed for high-resolution imaging and all-weather monitoring of Earth's surface.
• The satellite will provide crucial data for applications such as agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
• This mission further strengthens India's capabilities in remote sensing and enhances its space-based surveillance infrastructure.
• The launch reaffirms ISRO's commitment to providing critical space-based services for national development.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Space
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: PSLV is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle.
Earth Observation Satellites are used for monitoring Earth's environment and resources.
Sriharikota is the primary spaceport of ISRO.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites in India's national development and strategic interests.
Examine the evolution of ISRO's launch capabilities and its role in global space exploration.
articles.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket launching the EOS-08 satellite from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first successful launch of a satellite using PSLV was in 1994.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Unveils National Green Hydrogen Mission's Latest Roadmap
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change has released an updated roadmap for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, outlining key targets and implementation strategies.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels and achieving energy independence.
• The roadmap details plans for scaling up production capacity, creating demand through green hydrogen corridors, and fostering R&D in the sector.
• It also emphasizes the importance of policy incentives, private sector investment, and international collaborations to achieve mission objectives.
• Green hydrogen is seen as a critical component in India's climate change mitigation strategy and its commitment to net-zero emissions by 2070.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India aims for net-zero emissions by 2070.
The mission targets significant green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of green hydrogen as a sustainable energy source for India and the challenges in its large-scale adoption.
Discuss how the National Green Hydrogen Mission aligns with India's climate change commitments and energy security goals.
Suggest policy measures to accelerate investment and innovation in the green hydrogen sector.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of green hydrogen production process using renewable energy.
articles.trivia: India launched the National Green Hydrogen Mission in January 2023.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Privacy Rights in Landmark Data Protection Ruling
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a landmark judgment, reinforcing the fundamental right to privacy in the context of data protection.
• The ruling clarified specific provisions related to data processing, consent mechanisms, and the state's power to access personal data.
• It emphasized the need for a robust data protection framework that balances individual privacy with legitimate state interests and technological advancements.
• The judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for digital governance, industry practices, and individual rights in the digital age.
• This decision builds upon the earlier Puttaswamy judgment, which declared privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Constitution.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: The K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy as a fundamental right.
Article 21 of the Constitution guarantees the right to life and personal liberty.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is India's primary data protection law.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the right to privacy in India and the significance of the Supreme Court's latest ruling in strengthening data protection.
Discuss the challenges in balancing individual privacy rights with national security and economic interests in the digital era.
Suggest reforms needed in India's data governance framework to ensure robust data protection.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was enacted to provide for the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process such data for lawful purposes.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Anti-Naxal Operations in Chhattisgarh, Focus on Developmental Outreach
articles.explainer:
• The Union Home Ministry conducted a high-level review of anti-Naxal operations in Chhattisgarh, assessing security strategies and developmental initiatives.
• The meeting emphasized a two-pronged approach: strengthening security forces' capabilities and accelerating developmental outreach in Naxal-affected areas.
• Discussions focused on improving intelligence gathering, inter-state coordination, and providing essential services like roads, schools, and healthcare.
• The government reiterated its commitment to eliminating left-wing extremism (LWE) by addressing its root causes through socio-economic development.
• Special attention was given to integrating tribal communities into the mainstream economy and ensuring their participation in governance.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
prelimsPoints: Chhattisgarh is one of the states most affected by Naxalism.
The 'Samadhan' strategy is a multi-pronged approach to counter LWE.
Tribal communities often face displacement and marginalization in Naxal-affected regions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the complex interplay between underdevelopment and the spread of Naxalism in India, particularly in states like Chhattisgarh.
Discuss the effectiveness of government strategies, including both security operations and developmental initiatives, in combating Left-Wing Extremism.
Suggest measures for holistic development and inclusive governance to address the root causes of Naxalism.
articles.imageDescription: Map highlighting Naxal-affected districts in Chhattisgarh.
articles.trivia: The term 'Naxalite' originated from the village of Naxalbari in West Bengal, where a peasant uprising took place in 1967.

articles.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Phase, Hopes for Early Conclusion
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-United Kingdom Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their final and critical phase, with both sides expressing optimism for an early conclusion.
• The agreement aims to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment by reducing tariffs, streamlining customs procedures, and addressing non-tariff barriers.
• Key areas of discussion include goods, services, investment, intellectual property rights, and rules of origin.
• A successful FTA is expected to unlock new opportunities for businesses in both countries, particularly in sectors like automobiles, textiles, food & beverages, and digital services.
• The agreement is a strategic move for India to diversify its trade partnerships and for the UK to strengthen post-Brexit economic ties.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: An FTA aims to eliminate or reduce customs duties and non-tariff barriers between signatory countries.
India is negotiating FTAs with several countries and blocs, including the UK, EU, and Canada.
The UK exited the European Union in 2020 (Brexit).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic implications of the proposed India-UK Free Trade Agreement for both nations.
Discuss the challenges in negotiating comprehensive FTAs, particularly concerning sensitive sectors and intellectual property rights.
Evaluate India's broader strategy of engaging in bilateral and regional trade agreements.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United Kingdom displayed together.
articles.trivia: The UK is one of India's oldest and most significant trading partners.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Crosses Major Milestone in Healthcare Record Digitization
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has achieved a significant milestone, with millions of health records now linked to Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA).
• This nationwide initiative aims to create a seamless online platform for interoperable health data, enabling digital access to healthcare services.
• The mission facilitates the creation of unique ABHA IDs for citizens, linking their health records across various providers, including hospitals, clinics, and laboratories.
• It is designed to improve efficiency, transparency, and accessibility of healthcare services, particularly in remote and rural areas.
• The success of ABDM is crucial for realizing the vision of universal health coverage and a digitally empowered healthcare ecosystem in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched in September 2021 by the Prime Minister.
The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
ABHA ID is a 14-digit unique health identification number.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of digital health initiatives like ABDM in transforming India's healthcare delivery system and achieving universal health coverage.
Discuss the challenges associated with data privacy, digital literacy, and infrastructure in implementing large-scale digital health programs.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and effective utilization of digital health services across diverse populations.
articles.imageDescription: An infographic explaining the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

articles.headline: New Tiger Reserve Proposed in Western Ghats to Boost Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change is reviewing a proposal for a new tiger reserve in the ecologically sensitive Western Ghats region.
• The proposed reserve aims to provide an additional protected habitat for the endangered Bengal tiger and other wildlife, mitigating habitat fragmentation.
• The Western Ghats are a biodiversity hotspot, home to a significant population of tigers, elephants, and endemic species.
• The establishment of a new reserve would involve community consultations, relocation of villages (if necessary), and robust anti-poaching measures.
• Conservationists emphasize the importance of contiguous forest patches and corridor connectivity for the long-term survival of large carnivores.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and Ecology
Wildlife Protection Act
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats are one of the eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity in the world.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to conserve tigers in India.
The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in tiger conservation in India, particularly in biodiversity hotspots like the Western Ghats, and evaluate the effectiveness of establishing new tiger reserves.
Examine the human-wildlife conflict issues arising from conservation efforts and suggest sustainable solutions.
Analyze the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces.
articles.imageDescription: A Bengal tiger in its natural habitat in the Western Ghats.
articles.trivia: India is home to over 70% of the world's tiger population.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Electoral Funding Transparency
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee has submitted a report recommending significant reforms to enhance transparency in electoral funding in India.
• The recommendations include stricter disclosure norms for political parties, real-time reporting of donations, and a review of the electoral bond scheme.
• The panel emphasized the need to curb the influence of 'black money' in elections and ensure a level playing field for all political parties.
• It also suggested strengthening the Election Commission of India's (ECI) powers to regulate political financing and enforce compliance.
• The report aims to foster greater public trust in the electoral process and strengthen democratic accountability.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Transparency and accountability
prelimsPoints: The Election Commission of India (ECI) is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections.
Electoral bonds were introduced in 2018 to facilitate political donations.
The Representation of the People Act, 1951, governs the conduct of elections.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges posed by opaque electoral funding to democratic governance in India and the need for comprehensive reforms.
Critically evaluate the existing mechanisms for electoral financing, including the electoral bond scheme, in terms of transparency and accountability.
Suggest practical and constitutional measures to enhance transparency in political funding and strengthen the Election Commission's regulatory powers.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with the Indian Parliament in the background.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held in 1951-52.

articles.headline: IIT Researchers Develop Novel AI Model for Early Detection of Crop Diseases
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at a premier Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) have developed an innovative Artificial Intelligence (AI) model for the early and accurate detection of crop diseases.
• The AI-powered system utilizes image recognition and machine learning algorithms to analyze plant leaf images, identifying disease symptoms even at nascent stages.
• This technology aims to provide farmers with timely alerts, enabling proactive measures to prevent widespread crop loss and improve agricultural productivity.
• The model is designed to be accessible via smartphones, making advanced diagnostic tools available to a larger farming community, especially in remote areas.
• This breakthrough holds significant promise for enhancing food security and supporting sustainable agricultural practices in India.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
E-technology in the aid of farmers
Food processing and related industries in India- scope and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: AI is a branch of computer science that enables machines to perform human-like cognitive functions.
Precision agriculture involves using technology to improve crop yields and manage resources efficiently.
National e-Governance Plan in Agriculture (NeGPA) promotes ICT in agriculture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Artificial Intelligence and other emerging technologies in revolutionizing the agricultural sector in India, particularly in addressing challenges like crop diseases.
Examine the policy measures required to promote the adoption of such technologies by Indian farmers.
Analyze the implications of technological advancements for food security and farmer welfare in India.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer using a smartphone to scan plant leaves for disease detection.
articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice and wheat.

articles.headline: India Hosts Global Tourism Summit, Focus on Sustainable and Experiential Travel
articles.explainer:
• India is hosting the Global Tourism Summit, bringing together international stakeholders to discuss the future of the travel industry with a focus on sustainability and experiential tourism.
• The summit aims to showcase India's diverse cultural heritage, natural beauty, and adventure tourism potential to a global audience.
• Discussions include strategies for promoting responsible tourism, leveraging digital technologies, and enhancing connectivity to boost tourist arrivals.
• The government highlighted initiatives to support local communities through tourism, create employment, and preserve cultural and ecological sites.
• The event underscores India's growing ambition to become a premier global tourism destination, contributing significantly to its economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Tourism sector
mainsPoints: Examine the potential of India's tourism sector to drive economic growth, create employment, and promote cultural exchange.
Discuss the challenges in developing sustainable tourism in India and suggest policy interventions to overcome them.
Analyze how digital marketing and improved infrastructure can enhance India's global tourism appeal.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates attending the Global Tourism Summit in India.
articles.trivia: The 'Incredible India' campaign is a key initiative by the Ministry of Tourism to promote India as a tourist destination.

articles.headline: Election Commission Launches Nationwide Electoral Literacy Campaign for Youth
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) has launched a nationwide 'Electoral Literacy Campaign' aimed at educating young and first-time voters about the importance of democratic participation.
• The campaign utilizes digital platforms, social media, and educational workshops to disseminate information on voter registration, the electoral process, and ethical voting practices.
• It seeks to enhance civic engagement, combat misinformation, and foster a deeper understanding of democratic values among the youth.
• Special focus is on reaching out to college students and marginalized communities to ensure their active involvement in the electoral system.
• The initiative underscores ECI's commitment to strengthening India's democratic fabric through informed and responsible citizenry.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Role of civil services in a democracy
Elections
prelimsPoints: The Election Commission of India is an autonomous constitutional authority.
Article 324 of the Constitution provides for the Election Commission.
The voting age in India was reduced from 21 to 18 by the 61st Amendment Act, 1988.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of electoral literacy campaigns in strengthening democratic participation and addressing voter apathy, especially among the youth.
Examine the challenges faced by the ECI in ensuring free, fair, and inclusive elections in India.
Suggest measures to leverage technology and community engagement for effective voter education.
articles.imageDescription: Young people participating in an electoral literacy workshop.
articles.trivia: The first Chief Election Commissioner of India was Sukumar Sen.

articles.headline: India Participates in Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) Ministerial Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister participated in the Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) Ministerial Meeting, reiterating India's commitment to regional cooperation.
• The discussions focused on enhancing maritime security, promoting sustainable blue economy initiatives, and fostering cultural exchanges among member states.
• India emphasized the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, guided by international law and peaceful resolution of disputes.
• The meeting addressed common challenges such as climate change impact on coastal communities, illegal fishing, and disaster response coordination.
• IORA serves as a crucial platform for strengthening economic and strategic ties among countries bordering the Indian Ocean.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: IORA is an inter-governmental organization of coastal states bordering the Indian Ocean.
India is a founding member of IORA.
The 'SAGAR' (Security and Growth for All in the Region) vision underscores India's approach to the Indian Ocean Region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the Indian Ocean Region for India and the role of forums like IORA in promoting regional stability and cooperation.
Discuss India's 'Blue Economy' initiatives and their potential to drive sustainable development in the Indian Ocean region.
Examine the challenges to maritime security in the Indian Ocean and collaborative approaches to address them.
articles.imageDescription: Map of the Indian Ocean Rim Association member states.
articles.trivia: IORA was established in 1997 and its Secretariat is located in Ebene, Mauritius.

highlights: Gulzar Houz fire tragedy in Hyderabad claims 17 lives, raising concerns over urban safety and disaster preparedness.
India mourns the passing of nuclear science pioneer M. R. Srinivasan and renowned astrophysicist Jayant Narlikar.
Union Cabinet approves 'PM-GatiShakti 2.0' to accelerate multi-modal infrastructure development across the nation.
RBI maintains repo rate, adopting a cautious stance amidst global economic volatility and domestic inflation concerns.
Supreme Court delivers landmark judgment, reinforcing the fundamental right to privacy in the context of data protection.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's nuclear power program, consider the following statements regarding M. R. Srinivasan:
1. He was the Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission (AEC).
2. He played a key role in the development of India's indigenous Pressurized Heavy Water Reactors (PHWRs).
3. He was a strong proponent of utilizing thorium in India's three-stage nuclear power program.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: M. R. Srinivasan (1925-2025) was indeed a former Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission. He was instrumental in the design, construction, and operation of India's indigenous nuclear power plants, particularly the Pressurized Heavy Water Reactors (PHWRs). He was also a strong advocate for the three-stage nuclear power program, which aims to utilize India's vast thorium reserves for energy production. Therefore, all three statements are correct.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements about Jayant Narlikar:
1. He was an eminent astrophysicist known for his work on the Big Bang theory.
2. He founded the Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA).
3. He was awarded the Padma Vibhushan for his contributions to science.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Jayant Narlikar was an eminent astrophysicist (Statement 1 is partially incorrect as he was known for his work on the quasi-steady state theory, often seen as an alternative to the Big Bang theory, although he studied cosmology broadly). He founded the Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA) in Pune (Statement 2 is correct). He was awarded the Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honor, for his contributions to science (Statement 3 is correct). Therefore, statements 2 and 3 are correct.

prelimsMocks.question: PM-GatiShakti National Master Plan is primarily aimed at:
options: (a) Providing universal health coverage to all citizens.
(b) Integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure connectivity projects.
(c) Promoting digital literacy in rural areas.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure connectivity projects.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The PM-GatiShakti National Master Plan, launched in 2021, is an initiative for multi-modal connectivity infrastructure development. Its primary aim is to break departmental silos and ensure integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure projects across various ministries to improve logistics efficiency and reduce costs. Options (a), (c), and (d) relate to other government initiatives.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary function of the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)?
options: (b) Achieving the inflation target set by the government.
(c) Managing public debt and government borrowing.
(d) Supporting economic growth while keeping price stability in mind.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Managing public debt and government borrowing.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary mandate of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is to determine the policy interest rates (like the repo rate) to achieve the inflation target (currently 4% +/- 2%) while keeping in mind the objective of growth. Managing public debt and government borrowing is primarily a function of the Ministry of Finance, although RBI acts as the government's debt manager, it is not the primary function of the MPC.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), consider the following statements:
1. It aims to create a national digital health ecosystem.
2. It facilitates the creation of a unique Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) ID for citizens.
3. The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) was launched to create a national digital health ecosystem, making healthcare more accessible and efficient (Statement 1 is correct). It enables the creation of a unique Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) ID for citizens, linking their health records digitally (Statement 2 is correct). The National Health Authority (NHA) is indeed the implementing agency for ABDM (Statement 3 is correct). Therefore, all three statements are correct.

mainsMocks.question: The Gulzar Houz fire tragedy highlights critical gaps in urban safety and disaster management. Discuss the challenges faced by Indian cities in ensuring fire safety and effective disaster response, and suggest comprehensive measures to build resilient urban infrastructure. (150 words, 10 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Disaster and disaster management; Urbanization and its problems
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Gulzar Houz incident as a case study. Discuss challenges like old infrastructure, lack of enforcement of building codes, population density, inadequate emergency services, and climate change impacts. Suggest measures: updating building codes, regular safety audits, strengthening fire departments, public awareness, smart city planning, and inter-agency coordination.

mainsMocks.question: India's journey towards scientific self-reliance has been significantly shaped by the contributions of pioneers like M. R. Srinivasan and Jayant Narlikar. Evaluate their legacies in advancing India's nuclear energy and space/cosmology programs, respectively, and discuss the importance of indigenous research in national development. (250 words, 15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Energy; Space
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce M.R. Srinivasan's role in nuclear power (three-stage program, PHWRs) and Jayant Narlikar's in astrophysics (quasi-steady state theory, IUCAA). Highlight their contributions to self-reliance. Emphasize how indigenous research fosters innovation, reduces dependence, creates skilled workforce, and addresses national challenges (energy security, climate change, food security) leading to overall national development.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's latest ruling on data privacy, coupled with initiatives like the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, marks a significant step towards strengthening individual rights in the digital age. Analyze the evolution of the right to privacy in India and critically examine the challenges in implementing a robust data protection framework in a rapidly digitizing economy. (250 words, 15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—fundamental rights; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
mainsMocks.approach: Start with the K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) establishing privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21. Discuss the subsequent legislative efforts like the DPDP Act, 2023. Analyze challenges: balancing individual rights with state surveillance/national security, data localization, digital literacy, cross-border data flows, enforcement mechanisms, and industry compliance burdens. Conclude with the importance of a balanced approach.

quickBytes.text: Gulzar Houz is a historic octagonal fountain in Hyderabad's Old City, near Charminar.
M. R. Srinivasan was instrumental in establishing India's first nuclear power plant at Tarapur.
Jayant Narlikar, a Padma Vibhushan recipient, was known for the Hoyle-Narlikar theory of gravity.
The 61st Amendment Act, 1988, reduced the voting age in India from 21 to 18 years.
PM-GatiShakti aims to boost India's logistics performance index ranking.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises 6 members, 3 from RBI and 3 external.
Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using electricity from renewable energy sources.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the right to life and personal liberty.
The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
ISRO's PSLV is known as the 'workhorse' launch vehicle for its reliability and versatility.

GS2.headline: Former Chief Justice of Karnataka High Court, S. R. Nayak, Dies at 81
GS2.explainer:
• Justice S. R. Nayak, a distinguished jurist and former Chief Justice of the Karnataka High Court, passed away at the age of 81.
• Known for his integrity and commitment to justice, he had a long and impactful career in the Indian judiciary.
• Post-retirement, he also served as the Chairman of the Karnataka State Human Rights Commission, advocating for human rights protection.
• His judgments often reflected a deep understanding of constitutional law and social justice principles.
• Justice Nayak's demise marks the loss of a significant voice in India's legal fraternity.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Appointments to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Human Rights
prelimsPoints: High Court judges are appointed by the President of India.
State Human Rights Commissions are statutory bodies.
The Chief Justice of a High Court is appointed under Article 217 of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of former judges in upholding constitutional values and promoting human rights post-retirement.
Examine the functions and challenges faced by State Human Rights Commissions in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Portrait of Justice S. R. Nayak.
GS2.trivia: Justice Nayak was also known for his advocacy for legal aid to the underprivileged.

GS2.headline: India Pushes for Global South Representation at G20 Finance Ministers' Meeting in Brazil
GS2.explainer:
• India's Finance Minister participated in the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting hosted by Brazil, advocating for stronger representation of the Global South.
• The discussions focused on global economic stability, sustainable development financing, and multilateral debt restructuring for developing nations.
• India emphasized the need for inclusive economic policies and equitable access to resources and technology for emerging economies.
• The meeting also addressed challenges posed by climate change financing and the reform of international financial institutions.
• India's stance aligns with its ongoing efforts to amplify the voices and concerns of developing countries on global platforms.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Brazil holds the G20 presidency in 2024.
The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union.
India hosted the G20 Summit in 2023.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role as a voice for the Global South in multilateral forums like the G20.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for developing countries in shaping global economic governance.
Examine the importance of debt restructuring and climate finance for sustainable development in the Global South.
GS2.imageDescription: Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors attending the G20 meeting in Brazil.
GS2.trivia: The G20 represents about two-thirds of the world’s population, 85% of global GDP, and 75% of global trade.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Privacy Rights in Landmark Data Protection Ruling
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a landmark judgment, reinforcing the fundamental right to privacy in the context of data protection.
• The ruling clarified specific provisions related to data processing, consent mechanisms, and the state's power to access personal data.
• It emphasized the need for a robust data protection framework that balances individual privacy with legitimate state interests and technological advancements.
• The judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for digital governance, industry practices, and individual rights in the digital age.
• This decision builds upon the earlier Puttaswamy judgment, which declared privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Constitution.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: The K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy as a fundamental right.
Article 21 of the Constitution guarantees the right to life and personal liberty.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is India's primary data protection law.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of the right to privacy in India and the significance of the Supreme Court's latest ruling in strengthening data protection.
Discuss the challenges in balancing individual privacy rights with national security and economic interests in the digital era.
Suggest reforms needed in India's data governance framework to ensure robust data protection.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was enacted to provide for the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process such data for lawful purposes.

GS2.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Phase, Hopes for Early Conclusion
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-United Kingdom Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their final and critical phase, with both sides expressing optimism for an early conclusion.
• The agreement aims to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment by reducing tariffs, streamlining customs procedures, and addressing non-tariff barriers.
• Key areas of discussion include goods, services, investment, intellectual property rights, and rules of origin.
• A successful FTA is expected to unlock new opportunities for businesses in both countries, particularly in sectors like automobiles, textiles, food & beverages, and digital services.
• The agreement is a strategic move for India to diversify its trade partnerships and for the UK to strengthen post-Brexit economic ties.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: An FTA aims to eliminate or reduce customs duties and non-tariff barriers between signatory countries.
India is negotiating FTAs with several countries and blocs, including the UK, EU, and Canada.
The UK exited the European Union in 2020 (Brexit).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic implications of the proposed India-UK Free Trade Agreement for both nations.
Discuss the challenges in negotiating comprehensive FTAs, particularly concerning sensitive sectors and intellectual property rights.
Evaluate India's broader strategy of engaging in bilateral and regional trade agreements.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the United Kingdom displayed together.
GS2.trivia: The UK is one of India's oldest and most significant trading partners.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Crosses Major Milestone in Healthcare Record Digitization
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has achieved a significant milestone, with millions of health records now linked to Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA).
• This nationwide initiative aims to create a seamless online platform for interoperable health data, enabling digital access to healthcare services.
• The mission facilitates the creation of unique ABHA IDs for citizens, linking their health records across various providers, including hospitals, clinics, and laboratories.
• It is designed to improve efficiency, transparency, and accessibility of healthcare services, particularly in remote and rural areas.
• The success of ABDM is crucial for realizing the vision of universal health coverage and a digitally empowered healthcare ecosystem in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched in September 2021 by the Prime Minister.
The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
ABHA ID is a 14-digit unique health identification number.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of digital health initiatives like ABDM in transforming India's healthcare delivery system and achieving universal health coverage.
Discuss the challenges associated with data privacy, digital literacy, and infrastructure in implementing large-scale digital health programs.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and effective utilization of digital health services across diverse populations.
GS2.imageDescription: An infographic explaining the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Electoral Funding Transparency
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee has submitted a report recommending significant reforms to enhance transparency in electoral funding in India.
• The recommendations include stricter disclosure norms for political parties, real-time reporting of donations, and a review of the electoral bond scheme.
• The panel emphasized the need to curb the influence of 'black money' in elections and ensure a level playing field for all political parties.
• It also suggested strengthening the Election Commission of India's (ECI) powers to regulate political financing and enforce compliance.
• The report aims to foster greater public trust in the electoral process and strengthen democratic accountability.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Transparency and accountability
prelimsPoints: The Election Commission of India (ECI) is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections.
Electoral bonds were introduced in 2018 to facilitate political donations.
The Representation of the People Act, 1951, governs the conduct of elections.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges posed by opaque electoral funding to democratic governance in India and the need for comprehensive reforms.
Critically evaluate the existing mechanisms for electoral financing, including the electoral bond scheme, in terms of transparency and accountability.
Suggest practical and constitutional measures to enhance transparency in political funding and strengthen the Election Commission's regulatory powers.
GS2.imageDescription: A ballot box with the Indian Parliament in the background.
GS2.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held in 1951-52.

GS2.headline: Election Commission Launches Nationwide Electoral Literacy Campaign for Youth
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) has launched a nationwide 'Electoral Literacy Campaign' aimed at educating young and first-time voters about the importance of democratic participation.
• The campaign utilizes digital platforms, social media, and educational workshops to disseminate information on voter registration, the electoral process, and ethical voting practices.
• It seeks to enhance civic engagement, combat misinformation, and foster a deeper understanding of democratic values among the youth.
• Special focus is on reaching out to college students and marginalized communities to ensure their active involvement in the electoral system.
• The initiative underscores ECI's commitment to strengthening India's democratic fabric through informed and responsible citizenry.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Role of civil services in a democracy
Elections
prelimsPoints: The Election Commission of India is an autonomous constitutional authority.
Article 324 of the Constitution provides for the Election Commission.
The voting age in India was reduced from 21 to 18 by the 61st Amendment Act, 1988.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of electoral literacy campaigns in strengthening democratic participation and addressing voter apathy, especially among the youth.
Examine the challenges faced by the ECI in ensuring free, fair, and inclusive elections in India.
Suggest measures to leverage technology and community engagement for effective voter education.
GS2.imageDescription: Young people participating in an electoral literacy workshop.
GS2.trivia: The first Chief Election Commissioner of India was Sukumar Sen.

GS2.headline: India Participates in Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) Ministerial Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister participated in the Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) Ministerial Meeting, reiterating India's commitment to regional cooperation.
• The discussions focused on enhancing maritime security, promoting sustainable blue economy initiatives, and fostering cultural exchanges among member states.
• India emphasized the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, guided by international law and peaceful resolution of disputes.
• The meeting addressed common challenges such as climate change impact on coastal communities, illegal fishing, and disaster response coordination.
• IORA serves as a crucial platform for strengthening economic and strategic ties among countries bordering the Indian Ocean.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: IORA is an inter-governmental organization of coastal states bordering the Indian Ocean.
India is a founding member of IORA.
The 'SAGAR' (Security and Growth for All in the Region) vision underscores India's approach to the Indian Ocean Region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the Indian Ocean Region for India and the role of forums like IORA in promoting regional stability and cooperation.
Discuss India's 'Blue Economy' initiatives and their potential to drive sustainable development in the Indian Ocean region.
Examine the challenges to maritime security in the Indian Ocean and collaborative approaches to address them.
GS2.imageDescription: Map of the Indian Ocean Rim Association member states.
GS2.trivia: IORA was established in 1997 and its Secretariat is located in Ebene, Mauritius.

GS3.headline: Gulzar Houz Fire Tragedy: Calls for Enhanced Urban Safety Norms and Disaster Preparedness in Hyderabad
GS3.explainer:
• A devastating fire at Gulzar Houz in Hyderabad claimed at least 17 lives, primarily impacting commercial establishments and residential areas.
• The incident has sparked a critical debate on urban planning, fire safety regulations, and the enforcement mechanisms in densely populated historic areas.
• Experts highlight the need for modernized infrastructure, strict adherence to building codes, and effective disaster response protocols.
• The tragedy underscores vulnerabilities in old city areas, prompting calls for comprehensive safety audits and public awareness campaigns.
• Government authorities have initiated an inquiry into the cause of the fire and are reviewing existing safety guidelines.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Urbanization and its problems
Infrastructure development
prelimsPoints: Gulzar Houz is a historic fountain in Hyderabad's Old City.
Disaster Management Act, 2005 provides for effective management of disasters.
National Building Code of India (NBC) sets guidelines for building construction and safety.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the challenges in ensuring urban safety and disaster preparedness in India's rapidly growing cities, with reference to recent incidents.
Discuss the role of local bodies, state governments, and central agencies in implementing and enforcing fire safety norms.
Suggest measures to upgrade infrastructure and improve emergency response systems in congested urban areas.
GS3.imageDescription: Smoke billowing from the Gulzar Houz market area in Hyderabad following the fire.
GS3.trivia: Gulzar Houz, meaning 'fountain of roses', was built in the 17th century by the Qutb Shahi dynasty to provide water to the Charminar area.

GS3.headline: M. R. Srinivasan, Architect of India's Nuclear Power Program, Passes Away at 95
GS3.explainer:
• Dr. M. R. Srinivasan, a towering figure in India's nuclear energy landscape and former Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission, passed away at the age of 95.
• He played a pivotal role in the design, construction, and operation of India's indigenous nuclear power plants, including the Madras Atomic Power Station (MAPS).
• Srinivasan was instrumental in advancing India's three-stage nuclear power program, emphasizing self-reliance in nuclear technology.
• His leadership was crucial during the challenging phase of international sanctions following India's nuclear tests.
• He received the Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honor, for his contributions to science and engineering.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Energy
Nuclear technology
prelimsPoints: M. R. Srinivasan was Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission (AEC) from 1987 to 1990.
He contributed to the construction of India's first pressurized heavy water reactors (PHWRs).
India's three-stage nuclear power program aims to utilize thorium reserves.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the significance of India's indigenous nuclear power program in achieving energy security and technological self-reliance, with reference to contributions from pioneers like M.R. Srinivasan.
Discuss the current status and future prospects of India's nuclear energy sector, highlighting challenges and opportunities.
GS3.imageDescription: Portrait of Dr. M. R. Srinivasan, a distinguished nuclear scientist.
GS3.trivia: Dr. Srinivasan was also a member of the Planning Commission of India.

GS3.headline: Renowned Astrophysicist Jayant Narlikar Passes Away, Leaves Legacy in Cosmology Research
GS3.explainer:
• Padma Vibhushan recipient and eminent astrophysicist, Professor Jayant V. Narlikar, passed away at the age of 86.
• Known for his pioneering work in cosmology, Narlikar was a strong proponent of the quasi-steady state theory of the universe, developed with Fred Hoyle.
• He founded the Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA) in Pune, fostering astronomical research in India.
• Narlikar was also a prolific science popularizer and writer, making complex scientific concepts accessible to the public through his books and articles.
• His contributions significantly shaped India's presence in the global astrophysics community.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Space
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Jayant Narlikar was associated with the steady-state theory of the universe.
He founded the Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics (IUCAA) in Pune.
The Big Bang theory is the most widely accepted cosmological model.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of cosmological theories, highlighting the contributions of Indian scientists like Jayant Narlikar.
Examine the role of institutions like IUCAA in promoting scientific research and education in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Professor Jayant Narlikar, a distinguished astrophysicist.
GS3.trivia: Jayant Narlikar studied at the University of Cambridge and was a student of Fred Hoyle.

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves 'PM-GatiShakti 2.0' for Accelerated Infrastructure Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has given its nod to 'PM-GatiShakti 2.0', a comprehensive master plan aimed at accelerating multi-modal connectivity and infrastructure development across India.
• This enhanced version focuses on leveraging advanced technology, particularly AI and geospatial mapping, for integrated planning and project implementation.
• PM-GatiShakti 2.0 seeks to break silos between different ministries and departments, ensuring synchronized project execution and reducing logistical costs.
• The initiative aims to boost economic growth, create employment opportunities, and enhance India's global competitiveness by improving logistical efficiency.
• Special emphasis will be placed on last-mile connectivity and the development of critical infrastructure in underserved regions.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Growth and Development
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: PM-GatiShakti aims for integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure projects.
It uses a GIS-based platform for visualization and planning.
The initiative covers 16 ministries including Railways, Roads, Petroleum & Gas, Power, Telecom, Shipping, Aviation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of integrated infrastructure planning initiatives like PM-GatiShakti in achieving India's economic development goals.
Discuss the challenges in implementing large-scale infrastructure projects in India and how PM-GatiShakti 2.0 aims to address them.
GS3.imageDescription: Infographic illustrating the multi-modal connectivity vision of PM-GatiShakti.
GS3.trivia: The PM-GatiShakti National Master Plan was launched in October 2021.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Approach Amidst Global Economic Volatility
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI)'s Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• This decision reflects a cautious stance, balancing concerns over persistent inflation with the need to support economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest that the RBI is closely monitoring global commodity prices, geopolitical tensions, and domestic demand conditions.
• The central bank emphasized the resilience of the Indian economy but flagged potential risks from external headwinds.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.
The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting framework in India aims to keep inflation at 4% with a +/- 2% band.
mainsPoints: Examine the role of the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee in managing inflation and fostering economic growth in India.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in conducting monetary policy amidst global economic volatility and domestic supply-side pressures.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) headquarters in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The MPC consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-08'
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its latest Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08) aboard the PSLV-C58 rocket from Sriharikota.
• EOS-08 is equipped with advanced optical and synthetic aperture radar payloads, designed for high-resolution imaging and all-weather monitoring of Earth's surface.
• The satellite will provide crucial data for applications such as agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
• This mission further strengthens India's capabilities in remote sensing and enhances its space-based surveillance infrastructure.
• The launch reaffirms ISRO's commitment to providing critical space-based services for national development.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Space
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: PSLV is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle.
Earth Observation Satellites are used for monitoring Earth's environment and resources.
Sriharikota is the primary spaceport of ISRO.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites in India's national development and strategic interests.
Examine the evolution of ISRO's launch capabilities and its role in global space exploration.
GS3.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket launching the EOS-08 satellite from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's first successful launch of a satellite using PSLV was in 1994.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Environment Unveils National Green Hydrogen Mission's Latest Roadmap
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change has released an updated roadmap for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, outlining key targets and implementation strategies.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels and achieving energy independence.
• The roadmap details plans for scaling up production capacity, creating demand through green hydrogen corridors, and fostering R&D in the sector.
• It also emphasizes the importance of policy incentives, private sector investment, and international collaborations to achieve mission objectives.
• Green hydrogen is seen as a critical component in India's climate change mitigation strategy and its commitment to net-zero emissions by 2070.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India aims for net-zero emissions by 2070.
The mission targets significant green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of green hydrogen as a sustainable energy source for India and the challenges in its large-scale adoption.
Discuss how the National Green Hydrogen Mission aligns with India's climate change commitments and energy security goals.
Suggest policy measures to accelerate investment and innovation in the green hydrogen sector.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of green hydrogen production process using renewable energy.
GS3.trivia: India launched the National Green Hydrogen Mission in January 2023.

GS3.headline: Government Reviews Anti-Naxal Operations in Chhattisgarh, Focus on Developmental Outreach
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Home Ministry conducted a high-level review of anti-Naxal operations in Chhattisgarh, assessing security strategies and developmental initiatives.
• The meeting emphasized a two-pronged approach: strengthening security forces' capabilities and accelerating developmental outreach in Naxal-affected areas.
• Discussions focused on improving intelligence gathering, inter-state coordination, and providing essential services like roads, schools, and healthcare.
• The government reiterated its commitment to eliminating left-wing extremism (LWE) by addressing its root causes through socio-economic development.
• Special attention was given to integrating tribal communities into the mainstream economy and ensuring their participation in governance.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
prelimsPoints: Chhattisgarh is one of the states most affected by Naxalism.
The 'Samadhan' strategy is a multi-pronged approach to counter LWE.
Tribal communities often face displacement and marginalization in Naxal-affected regions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the complex interplay between underdevelopment and the spread of Naxalism in India, particularly in states like Chhattisgarh.
Discuss the effectiveness of government strategies, including both security operations and developmental initiatives, in combating Left-Wing Extremism.
Suggest measures for holistic development and inclusive governance to address the root causes of Naxalism.
GS3.imageDescription: Map highlighting Naxal-affected districts in Chhattisgarh.
GS3.trivia: The term 'Naxalite' originated from the village of Naxalbari in West Bengal, where a peasant uprising took place in 1967.

GS3.headline: New Tiger Reserve Proposed in Western Ghats to Boost Conservation Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change is reviewing a proposal for a new tiger reserve in the ecologically sensitive Western Ghats region.
• The proposed reserve aims to provide an additional protected habitat for the endangered Bengal tiger and other wildlife, mitigating habitat fragmentation.
• The Western Ghats are a biodiversity hotspot, home to a significant population of tigers, elephants, and endemic species.
• The establishment of a new reserve would involve community consultations, relocation of villages (if necessary), and robust anti-poaching measures.
• Conservationists emphasize the importance of contiguous forest patches and corridor connectivity for the long-term survival of large carnivores.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and Ecology
Wildlife Protection Act
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats are one of the eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity in the world.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to conserve tigers in India.
The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in tiger conservation in India, particularly in biodiversity hotspots like the Western Ghats, and evaluate the effectiveness of establishing new tiger reserves.
Examine the human-wildlife conflict issues arising from conservation efforts and suggest sustainable solutions.
Analyze the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces.
GS3.imageDescription: A Bengal tiger in its natural habitat in the Western Ghats.
GS3.trivia: India is home to over 70% of the world's tiger population.

GS3.headline: IIT Researchers Develop Novel AI Model for Early Detection of Crop Diseases
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers at a premier Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) have developed an innovative Artificial Intelligence (AI) model for the early and accurate detection of crop diseases.
• The AI-powered system utilizes image recognition and machine learning algorithms to analyze plant leaf images, identifying disease symptoms even at nascent stages.
• This technology aims to provide farmers with timely alerts, enabling proactive measures to prevent widespread crop loss and improve agricultural productivity.
• The model is designed to be accessible via smartphones, making advanced diagnostic tools available to a larger farming community, especially in remote areas.
• This breakthrough holds significant promise for enhancing food security and supporting sustainable agricultural practices in India.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
E-technology in the aid of farmers
Food processing and related industries in India- scope and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: AI is a branch of computer science that enables machines to perform human-like cognitive functions.
Precision agriculture involves using technology to improve crop yields and manage resources efficiently.
National e-Governance Plan in Agriculture (NeGPA) promotes ICT in agriculture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Artificial Intelligence and other emerging technologies in revolutionizing the agricultural sector in India, particularly in addressing challenges like crop diseases.
Examine the policy measures required to promote the adoption of such technologies by Indian farmers.
Analyze the implications of technological advancements for food security and farmer welfare in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer using a smartphone to scan plant leaves for disease detection.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice and wheat.

GS3.headline: India Hosts Global Tourism Summit, Focus on Sustainable and Experiential Travel
GS3.explainer:
• India is hosting the Global Tourism Summit, bringing together international stakeholders to discuss the future of the travel industry with a focus on sustainability and experiential tourism.
• The summit aims to showcase India's diverse cultural heritage, natural beauty, and adventure tourism potential to a global audience.
• Discussions include strategies for promoting responsible tourism, leveraging digital technologies, and enhancing connectivity to boost tourist arrivals.
• The government highlighted initiatives to support local communities through tourism, create employment, and preserve cultural and ecological sites.
• The event underscores India's growing ambition to become a premier global tourism destination, contributing significantly to its economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Tourism sector
mainsPoints: Examine the potential of India's tourism sector to drive economic growth, create employment, and promote cultural exchange.
Discuss the challenges in developing sustainable tourism in India and suggest policy interventions to overcome them.
Analyze how digital marketing and improved infrastructure can enhance India's global tourism appeal.
GS3.imageDescription: Delegates attending the Global Tourism Summit in India.
GS3.trivia: The 'Incredible India' campaign is a key initiative by the Ministry of Tourism to promote India as a tourist destination.

sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
